zh.Penrhyn Castle, Caernarfonshire, Wales: 威爾士凱爾納方史爾卡素
zh.lifeSummary: 當Marged ferch Robert於1304年出生在威爾士福林爾時，她的父親Robert ap Iorwerth37歲，母親Adles verch Ithel Vychan40歲。她於1325年與Madog ap Gronwy在英國威爾斯安格萊結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.Penrhyn Castle, Caernarfonshire, Wales: 威尔士凱爾納方史爾卡素
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Marged ferch Robert于1304年出生在威尔士福林爾时，她的父亲Robert ap Iorwerth37岁，母亲Adles verch Ithel Vychan40岁。她于1325年与Madog ap Gronwy在英国威尔斯安格萊结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Marcel, Gilles, Normand, Armand, Lucien, Pierre, Emile, Michel, Edouard, Gaston, Yves.

French, Walloon, English, German, Dutch, Flemish, and Breton (of French origin); Hungarian (Róbert): from the ancient Germanic personal name Ro(d)bert (Middle English Robert, Old French Ro(d)bert), composed of the elements hrōd ‘fame, renown’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This is found occasionally in Anglo-Saxon England before the Conquest, but in the main it was introduced into England by the Normans and quickly became popular among all classes of society. Pet forms and diminutives are found from an early date, Robin being particularly common. Compare Robair , Robar , Robare , and Rober .
In some cases also an Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Jewish surnames.

variants.name: Rabbitt
meanings: English: from the Middle English male personal name Rabbode, Radboda (ancient Germanic Radbodo, from elements meaning ‘counsel’ + ‘messenger’).
English: from the personal name Robet or Robot, diminutives of Rob (see Robert ).
English: nickname from late Middle English rabet ‘young rabbit’. This word is not recorded until c. 1398, the usual Middle English word for ‘rabbit’ being cony (see Coney ).

variants.name: McRobbie
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeenshire): patronymic meaning ‘son of Robbie’, from the personal name Robbie, a Scottish pet form of Robert , with the Gaelic prefix mac ‘son of’.

variants.name: Rabjohn
meanings: English (Devon, Essex): from the Middle English personal name Robechon (of Norman French origin: Old Picard Robichon, Old French Robeçun), a double diminutive of Rob, a pet form of Robert , altered by folk etymology to Robjohn and Rabjohn.

variants.name: Nabb
meanings: English (Lancashire): topographic name for someone who lived by a nab, Middle English nabbe ‘hillock, knoll’ (Old Norse nabbi ‘projecting peak, hill’), or a habitational name from any of the many minor places in northern and eastern England named with this word, for example Whalley Nab in Blackburn (Lancashire), Nab Scar in Rydal (Westmorland), and The Nab in Burgh Saint Margaret (Norfolk).
English (Lancashire): alternatively, it may be from the Middle English personal names Nabb and Napp, which are probably parallels to Nobb and Nopp, pet forms of Robert . Compare Nobbs .
Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac an Aba(dh) ‘son of the abbot’, see McNab .

variants.name: Probert
meanings: Welsh: Anglicized form of Welsh ap Roppert ‘son of Robert’(see Robert ).

variants.name: Rabb
meanings: Scottish and English (Lancashire): from the Middle English personal name Rab(b), a pet form of Robert with unrounding of the first vowel. Compare Robb .
Hungarian: variant of   Rab  .

variants.name: Rapson
meanings: English: patronymic from the Middle English personal name Rab(b) (a pet form of Robert with unrounding of the first vowel) + -son, with devoicing of /b/ to /p/. Compare Robson and Rabb .

variants.name: McCobb
meanings: Irish: from Mac Hob, ‘son of Hob ‘, an English pet form of Robert . Compare Hobson .

variants.name: McRoberts
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Roibeirt, a patronymic from a Gaelic form of the personal name Robert , with English excrescent -s.

variants.name: Robin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Andre, Emile, Marcel, Achille, Armand, Arnaud, Camille, Julien, Michel, Normand.
French, Breton, Scottish, English, and German: from the personal name Robin, a pet form of Robert , composed of the short form Rob + the hypocoristic suffix -in.
Slovenian: topographic name derived from rob ‘edge, border’ (see Robar ).

nameMeanings.name: Marged
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: equivalent of Margaret .

variants.name: Margaret
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Mared
meanings: Simplified form of   Marged .

nameMeanings.name: ferch
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: variant of   Ferg  . Compare   Verch  .
Probably also an Americanized form of German Förtsch (see Foertsch ).
Probably also an Americanized form of German Förch (see Foerch ).

variants.name: Ferg
meanings: From the vocabulary word denoting the plant (Old English fearn). Modern use of this word as a given name dates from the 19th century, when it is also recorded occasionally as a boy's name in the United States. In some instances the given name may be a transferred use of the surname derived in the medieval period from the vocabulary word.

en.lifeSummary: When Marged ferch Robert was born in 1304, in Flintshire, Wales, her father, Robert ap Iorwerth, was 37 and her mother, Adles verch Ithel Vychan, was 40. She married Madog ap Gronwy in 1325, in Anglesey, Wales, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Marged ferch Robert est née en 1304, à Flintshire, Galles, son père, Robert ap Iorwerth, avait 37 ans et sa mère, Adles verch Ithel Vychan, avait 40 ans. Elle a épousé Madog ap Gronwy en 1325, à Anglesey, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Als Marged ferch Robert 1304 in Flintshire, Wales geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Robert ap Iorwerth 37 und ihre Mutter Adles verch Ithel Vychan 40. Sie hat 1325 in Anglesey, Wales, United Kingdom Madog ap Gronwy geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter.

ht., Anglesey, , Wales: Anglesey, Pays de Galles, United Kingdom

it.lifeSummary: Quando Marged ferch Robert è nata il nel 1304 nel Flintshire, Galles, sua padre, Robert ap Iorwerth, aveva 37 anni e sua madre, Adles verch Ithel Vychan, aveva 40 anni. Lei sposato Madog ap Gronwy nel 1325, nel Anglesey, Galles, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia.

ko.lifeSummary: Marged ferch Robert(이)가 1304년 웨일스 플린츠히르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert ap Iorwerth(은)는 37세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Adles verch Ithel Vychan(은)는 40세였다. 그녀는 1325년 영국 웨일스 앤글레시에서 Madog ap Gronwy(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Marged ferch Robert nasceu em 1304, em Flintshire, Gales, seu pai, Robert ap Iorwerth, tinha 37 anos e sua mãe, Adles verch Ithel Vychan, tinha 40 anos. Ela casou-se com Madog ap Gronwy em 1325, em Anglesey, Gales, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Marged ferch Robert родилась в 1304 г. в местности флинцхир, Уэльс, её отцу по имени Robert ap Iorwerth было 37 лет, а матери, которую звали Adles verch Ithel Vychan, 40. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Madog ap Gronwy в 1325 г. в местности англеси, Уэльс, Великобритания. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Marged ferch Robert nació en 1304, en Flintshire, Gales, su papá, Robert ap Iorwerth, tenía 37 años y su mamá, Adles verch Ithel Vychan, tenía 40 años. Se casó con Madog ap Gronwy en 1325, en Isla Oscura, Gales, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija.
